_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Local batch: Fani Willis (Fulton Co GA DA), Jason Williams (Orleans Parish LA DA), Mary Moriarty (Hennepin Co MN Attorney), Dante Anderson (Charlotte NC D1) — plus Joe Baldacci (ME, federal-tier ME-02 candidate who matched the local queue via his Bangor-councilor history). Evidence-based finalization: parallel web research 2026-06-10; every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence. Major corrections: Jason Williams record falsely claimed a 2025 tax guilty plea — he was ACQUITTED on all 10 federal counts July 2022. Moriarty is not seeking re-election (announced Aug 2025). Baldacci's ME-02 primary (2026-06-09) is in an unresolved ranked-choice count.

profile.religion: Professing Christian (regularly speaks in AME church settings; no denomination membership documented)
profile.education: Howard University (BA political science); Emory University School of Law (JD 1996)
profile.background: Career Fulton County ADA (100+ jury trials); South Fulton municipal judge 2019; first woman elected Fulton DA (2020). Led the APS test-cheating RICO and YSL/Young Thug RICO prosecutions.

fani-willis.notes_append: [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Fulton DA, re-elected Nov 2024 over Republican Courtney Kramer, term through 2028; disqualified from the Trump case (final Sept 2025) but not removed from office. Documented profile: a 'tough-on-crime liberal' — police-union-endorsed in 2020, aggressive gang prosecution (YSL RICO; asked chiefs to prioritize gang-act charges adding 5-15 years; helped write anti-gang legislation), reversed her campaign stance to seek the death penalty in the 2021 spa shootings. Contra: pledged within hours of Dobbs not to prosecute under GA's heartbeat law; created an office LGBTQ+ Advisory Committee and hosted a Pride Month vigil; opposed GA's 2024 cash-bail expansion law. Frequently professes Christ publicly from church pulpits as DA. No documented Soros/Justice & Public Safety PAC funding (police-union-backed; the 'Soros DA' label appears only in advocacy pieces). Budget fights with her own county commission (demanded more funding, threatened to sue) and prolonged resistance to GA Senate oversight subpoenas (testified Dec 2025) are documented but map to no rubric cell as scored. NO EVIDENCE on schools, voter ID, sanctuary/ICE, or gun-rights cells — null, not penalized.

1.note: Hours after Dobbs (June 2022) pledged not to prosecute under GA's heartbeat law: 'I will not be using precious tax dollars... to pursue prosecutions based on women's personal healthcare choices.' Opposite of abolition advocacy.

0.note: No statement found on when life begins.

4.note: Created an LGBTQ+ Advisory Committee inside the DA's office and hosted a Pride Month vigil — official office promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

0.note: No statement found on the definition of marriage.

2.note: No statement found on transgender ideology/biological sex specifically.

0.note: Publicly professes Christ while holding public office, from church pulpits: 'Jesus is the only source I can rely on every time and all the time' (Big Bethel AME, Jan 2024); 'Me and my God, we talk like regular folks.'

2.note: Demanded a larger county allocation and threatened to sue her own Board of Commissioners over the FY2025 budget — an allocation fight, not a deficit-spending position; left null.

4.note: No position found on private election funding; her election-case work (2020 RICO indictment, later disqualification for appearance of impropriety) maps to no rubric cell.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond routine governance: 2020 campaign endorsed and funded by the Atlanta police union; ran on clearing the police-misconduct backlog; asked police chiefs to prioritize gang-act charges and helped write anti-gang legislation.

1.note: Publicly opposed Georgia's 2024 cash-bail expansion law ('this law will create overcrowding and new victims') and considered a legal challenge; praised by the ACLU for it.

2.note: Reversed her campaign stance to seek the death penalty in the 2021 spa shootings ('warrants the ultimate penalty'); sought 45 years for Young Thug; pretrial diversion program includes restitution.

3.note: Treats violent crime as a prosecution problem, not a public-health excuse: blamed gangs for 70-90% of crime, prioritized sentencing enhancements, led the longest criminal trial in GA history (YSL RICO).

4.note: No Romans-13-style statement on the magistrate's duty found; her death-penalty reversal is scored at q2.

0.note: Resisted the state Prosecuting Attorneys Qualifications Commission (SB 92) and GA Senate subpoenas for over a year — prosecutorial-independence conflict, but SB 92 is not an unconstitutional mandate; left null.

profile.background: Criminal-defense attorney; longtime Innocence Project New Orleans volunteer; at-large New Orleans City Councilman before DA. Indicted 2020 on 10 federal tax-fraud counts; ACQUITTED on all counts July 2022.

soros.note: New Orleans (Orleans Parish) District Attorney. The Soros-funded Louisiana Justice & Public Safety PAC spent in support of his 2020 race (Soros gave $220K+ to the PAC, which spent against his opponent; policedefense.org sorosmap lists $500K+). CORRECTION 2026-06-10: prior note claimed a 2025 misdemeanor tax guilty plea — false. He was indicted in 2020 on 10 federal felony tax counts and acquitted on ALL counts in July 2022.
soros.descriptor: The Open Society Foundations and the Democracy PAC vehicles tied to George Soros / Alex Soros direct hundreds of millions toward progressive prosecutors, judges, and ballot measures. RESOLUTE Citizen treats documented contributions as foreign-linked dark-money funding hostile to the historically orthodox Christian moral order. The specific failure mode is on life q4 — Soros-funded DAs systematically decline to prosecute attacks on pro-life pregnancy centers and refuse to enforce state abortion restrictions.

jason-williams.notes_set: Orleans Parish DA since Jan 2021 (elected Dec 2020, defeating Keva Landrum); term through Dec 31, 2027; no declared re-election bid found. CORRECTION 2026-06-10: a prior note claimed he 'pleaded guilty in 2025 to misdemeanor tax charges' — FALSE. He was indicted in 2020 on 10 federal felony tax-fraud counts and a federal jury ACQUITTED him on all counts in July 2022 (his tax preparer and former law associates pleaded guilty in related cases). [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE refinement] Documented profile: archetypal Soros-era progressive prosecutor who later executed tough-on-crime reversals — pledged post-Dobbs not to prosecute under Louisiana's abortion ban; first year dismissed 1,859 open felonies and stopped prosecuting most low-level drug possession (ACLU questionnaire pledged 'decline & divert' and alternatives to cash bail); Civil Rights Division vacated ~100 sentences. From 2021-23 reversed course: resumed charging some juveniles as adults ('the position to never charge juveniles as adults was the wrong one'), revived the habitual-offender law he campaigned against, rebuked his own deputy for refusing Mardi Gras gun charges, and ceded some cases to the state AG under Gov. Landry. NOLA homicides hit a 50-year low in 2025 (121), which he claims partial credit for; watchdog MCC said public safety 'suffered' in his first two years. Sanctuary-aligned: ICE cooperation only for violent-crime suspects. Opposed Louisiana's 2024 permitless-carry law for New Orleans and backed the French Quarter carve-out workaround.

1.note: Pledged not to prosecute abortion providers or patients under Louisiana's post-Dobbs ban: 'It would not be wise or prudent to shift our priority... to investigating the choices women make with regard to their own bodies.'

0.note: No statement found on when life begins.

3.note: Joined faith leaders to pray publicly for peace (Feb 2026) and campaigned in churches — participation, not a policy position on public prayer; left null.

0.note: Mixed: publicly rebuked his own deputy for refusing Mardi Gras gun charges and touts an 85.7% homicide conviction rate, but his broader record is mass dismissals; no clear affirmative pro-police advocacy or defund stance found — null.

1.note: Implemented the policies this cell opposes: ACLU questionnaire pledge to 'decline & divert' with alternatives to cash bail; dismissed 1,859 open felonies in year one; stopped prosecuting most low-level drug possession. Partial later reversals noted but the documented policy record stands.

2.note: Mixed: post-conviction unit freed 86 people (49 lifers) — framed as wrongful-conviction relief — while he later revived the habitual-offender law; no clean restitution/proportionality position — null.

3.note: Explicitly reversed soft-on-violent-crime positions: 'the position to never charge juveniles as adults was the wrong one' (resumed adult charges, May 2021); revived the habitual-offender law (Mar 2023).

0.note: Largely cooperated with the state (ceded State Police-initiated cases to the AG; 'allyship' with Gov. Landry); his abortion non-enforcement defiance of state pressure is scored at life q1 — null here.

2.note: Embraced New Orleans' sanctuary posture — ICE cooperation only for violent-crime suspects: 'I don't think the tolerance level, or the way that New Orleans shows love, is going to change simply because of a change in the administration.'

1.note: No position found on deportation policy itself.

0.note: Opposed Louisiana's 2024 permitless (constitutional) carry law for New Orleans, pleaded for a French Quarter carve-out, then backed the legal workaround declaring the 8th District police station a 'school' to keep the Quarter gun-free.

set.office: U.S. Representative ME-02 (2026 D primary candidate · open Golden seat · sitting Maine State Senator SD-9, Bangor)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from his real Maine Senate record + campaign positions. ME-02 D primary (2026-06-09) UNRESOLVED: no one reached 50%, race went to a multi-day ranked-choice count; early returns had Jordan Wood 33.4%, Baldacci 31%, Dunlap 25.6%, Loud 9.9%; pre-election RCV simulation favored Baldacci on transfers. Update candidacy_status when called.
profile.religion: Raised Roman Catholic (Italian/Lebanese-American family; youngest of eight)
profile.education: University of Maine (BA); University of Maine School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Attorney; four-term Bangor city councilor (twice mayor/chair); three-term Maine state senator (SD-9, since Dec 2020); brother of former Gov. John Baldacci. DCCC 'Red to Blue' endorsee.

joe-baldacci.notes_set: Joe Baldacci — sitting Maine State Senator (SD-9, Bangor; term ends Dec 2026, not seeking re-election to it) running for the open ME-02 U.S. House seat (Jared Golden declined a fifth term; Paul LePage is the likely GOP nominee). The 2026-06-09 D primary went to a ranked-choice count: early returns Wood 33.4% / Baldacci 31% / Dunlap 25.6% / Loud 9.9%; pre-election SurveyUSA/FairVote RCV simulation had Baldacci winning the final round 56-44 on transfers. RESULT PENDING — update when called. [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE refinement] Scored on his real legislative record: 100% Planned Parenthood Maine scorecard every year since Dobbs (incl. LD 1619, 2023 post-viability abortion expansion); endorsed by Reproductive Freedom for All; campaigns on 'Defending Reproductive Rights' and LGBTQ+ rights. Voted to reject 2023 bills restoring vaccine religious exemptions; 2020: called himself the 'only candidate... who supports basic science' re mandates. Notable party break on guns: voted NO on the post-Lewiston LD 2224 package (background checks + yellow-flag expansion) over civil-liberty objections, and opposes an AR-15 ban — but supports magazine and bump-stock restrictions. Supports congressional war-powers approval before military action; Ukraine aid contingent on Ukrainian preferences. NO EVIDENCE found on voter ID (incl. Maine's 2025 Question 1), the 2025 red-flag referendum, immigration, school choice, or sound money.

1.note: 100% Planned Parenthood Maine scorecard every year since Dobbs, including LD 1619 (2023 post-viability abortion expansion); endorsed by Reproductive Freedom for All; campaigns on 'Defending Reproductive Rights.'

4.note: RFFA endorsement documented, but no abortion-industry PAC *funding* documented — null on the funding cell.
Campaigns on support for LGBTQ+ rights, citing his state senate record (Maine Public 2026 candidate profile).

2.note: Senate Democrats unanimously killed the 2025 trans-athlete bills, but his individual roll call was not retrievable — null rather than inferred.

1.note: Voted with the Senate majority to reject 2023 bills restoring religious exemptions to school vaccine mandates; dismissed exemption arguments as 'misinformation.'
No personal stance found on Maine's Nov 2025 voter-ID referendum (Question 1) or voter ID generally.

Split within this cell: voted NO on LD 2224 (yellow-flag expansion + background checks — 'I can't vote for a measure that would allow police to take someone in a mental health crisis into custody even if they haven't committed any crimes') and opposes an AR-15 ban, BUT supports high-capacity-magazine and bump-stock restrictions. Mixed evidence — null.

0.note: Stated position: opposes U.S. military action without congressional approval; opposes war with Iran absent clear U.S. security interests.

3.note: J Street PAC lists him as a candidate (page unavailable, listing only); no donation acceptance documented — null.

0.note: Pro-vaccine-mandate record: voted to reject exemption-restoration bills (2023); 2020 Facebook post backing schools/employers that mandate vaccination, calling himself the 'only candidate for state senate in District 9 who supports basic science.'

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized. Announced Aug 2025 she will NOT seek a second term; open 2026 race to succeed her (Frazier, Nguyen, Folk, Pelikan; Aug 2026 nonpartisan primary).
profile.education: Macalester College (BA 1986); University of Minnesota Law School (JD 1989)
profile.background: Career Hennepin County public defender; Chief Public Defender 2014-2020 (board declined reappointment, Sept 2020). First openly LGBTQ Hennepin County Attorney.

mary-moriarty.notes_set: Hennepin County Attorney since Jan 2023 (elected Nov 2022). Announced Aug 2025 she will NOT seek re-election; term ends after 2026 — the open race to succeed her is widely framed as a referendum on her reform-prosecutor tenure. [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE refinement] Documented progressive-prosecutor record: pledged to decline prosecution of abortion-related offenses and 'uphold the principles Roe stood for'; office issued an official Trans Day of Visibility statement and marched at Pride (Stonewall DFL / OutFront MN / LGBTQ Victory Fund endorsed, 2022); ended bail requests for several low-level offenses; stopped prosecuting most felonies from low-level traffic stops; juvenile no-prison plea deals in the Zaria McKeever murder triggered Gov. Walz reassigning the case to AG Ellison (second such takeover in ~30 years — she called it 'undemocratic'); a judge took the rare step of rejecting her no-prison plea in the Braveheart murder case; charged trooper Ryan Londregan with murder then dropped all charges (June 2024) — Walz said he had planned to remove her from the case; her Apr 2025 policy telling prosecutors to consider defendants' 'racial identity' in plea negotiations drew a DOJ civil-rights probe (opened May 2025; closed Feb 2026 after DOJ said the office 'corrected a policy that clearly violated the Constitution' — her office called the probe a 'stunt'). 2026: charged two on-duty ICE officers with assault during the MN immigration crackdown (first known such charges); charging policy explicitly weighs 'immigration impacts'; office eased U/T-visa certification. Self-described 'public health perspective' on the criminal legal system. 2022 race funding: ~$294K direct; outside money incl. TakeAction MN ($231K) and People Over Prosecution ($225K; largest donor Tides Advocacy) — Soros-network-aligned per critics, though no direct Soros PAC dollar figure was verified. NO EVIDENCE on election integrity, religious liberty, school/parental-rights, or explicit gun-control advocacy — null.

1.note: Campaign vision: committed to 'uphold the principles Roe stood for,' protect abortion access, and join pledges declining prosecution of abortion-related offenses.

0.note: No statement found on when life begins.
Openly queer; accepted endorsements from Stonewall DFL, OutFront Minnesota, and the LGBTQ Victory Fund — single-issue endorsements that are hard evidence against the man-woman marriage position.

2.note: Issued an official office statement for Trans Day of Visibility (2024).

4.note: Office promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy: official TDOV statement; office marched at Pride (her own post).

0.note: Documented adversarial relationship with law enforcement: police/trooper unions said her policies put law enforcement in a 'state of crisis' and sought her removal from the Londregan murder prosecution of a state trooper; LE leaders said her traffic-stop non-prosecution policy would 'embolden criminals.'

1.note: Ended bail requests for several low-level offenses ('it's wealth-based'); stopped prosecuting most felonies from low-level traffic stops; no-prison juvenile pleas in a murder case triggered a state takeover.

2.note: A judge took the rare step of rejecting her no-prison plea offer in the Braveheart murder case over the victim family's objection; victim families protested her juvenile plea deals; her Apr 2025 policy directed prosecutors to weigh defendants' 'racial identity' in plea negotiations — contrary to proportional, equal punishment.

3.note: Her own framing: 'I believe in addressing the entire criminal legal system from a public health perspective.'

4.note: No statement on the magistrate's duty; the pattern is scored at q0-q3.

0.note: Asserted prosecutorial independence against the Walz/Ellison case takeovers ('undemocratic... dangerous precedent') — but a lawful gubernatorial reassignment power is not an unconstitutional state mandate; null.

1.note: Office policy requires prosecutors to 'meaningfully consider all collateral consequences at every stage of every case,' explicitly including 'immigration impacts' — charging calibrated to avoid deportation consequences, the opposite of mandatory-deportation support.

2.note: Sanctuary-aligned posture: charged two on-duty ICE officers with assault during the 2026 MN immigration crackdown (first known such charges; DOJ asserted Supremacy Clause immunity); office eased U/T-visa certification for non-citizen crime victims.

1.note: No explicit gun-control advocacy found; her traffic-stop policy reduces gun-possession prosecutions (she countered guns appear in <0.5% of such stops) — scored under public_justice, null here.

profile.education: MIT (BS Electrical Engineering + Cinema Studies); NYU Tisch (MFA); Harvard Business School (MBA)
profile.background: Charlotte native raised in public housing near Eastland; 25+ years in corporate America managing large budgets. First elected to council Sept 2022; Mayor Pro Tem Dec 2023-Dec 2025. Former Equality NC regional representative; 4 years on the Charlotte LGBT Chamber of Commerce board.

dante-anderson.notes_set: Charlotte City Council District 1 (D). Roster verified 2026-06-10: won a third term — ~68% in the Sept 2025 D primary, unopposed in the Nov 2025 general; term ends Dec 2027. Served as Mayor Pro Tem Dec 2023-Dec 2025 (council chose James Mitchell in Dec 2025); has publicly weighed in on replacing Mayor Lyles and teased a 2026 announcement — possible mayoral ambitions. [2026-06-10 RESOLUTE refinement] Documented profile: chairs the council Safety Committee and affirmatively championed 10% CMPD raises in the FY2027 budget debate ('I can't support a position where we are going to reduce the CMPD officers down to seven percent') — paired with data-driven hotspot violence-intervention programs ('Queen City Safe and Sound'). Voted YES on the FY2027 budget with its 1.89-cent property-tax increase (9-2, June 2026), the $650M Bank of America Stadium subsidy (7-3, June 2024), and the FY2024 $3.3B budget. Openly LGBTQ+ (former Equality NC regional rep; LPAC-endorsed). As Mayor Pro Tem publicly rebuked the Nov 2025 federal 'Operation Charlotte's Web' immigration surge ('They say criminals, but it looks like they're just pulling people off the street'). NO EVIDENCE on abortion, schools, faith, election integrity, or guns — null, not penalized.

0.note: Openly LGBTQ+; served as a Charlotte regional representative for Equality NC (whose agenda includes marriage equality) and accepted the LPAC endorsement ('LGBTQ Equality. Women's Equality. Social Justice.').

4.note: Personal advocacy roles promoting LGBTQ+ identity in public policy: Equality NC regional representative, 4 years on the Charlotte LGBT Chamber board, LPAC-endorsed.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2027 budget including a 1.89-cent property-tax increase (9-2) and YES on $650M in public money for Bank of America Stadium renovations (7-3); also backs the proposed 1-cent transit sales tax.

0.note: Affirmative advocacy beyond a routine budget vote: as Safety Committee chair she argued in debate for the full 10% CMPD raise — 'I can't support a position where we are going to reduce the CMPD officers down to seven percent.' No defund advocacy found.

3.note: Champions public-health-style violence interruption ('instead of simply ramping up patrols across the board') but pairs it with police raises and has no prosecutorial role — not punishment avoidance; null.

2.note: As Mayor Pro Tem, officially rebuked the Nov 2025 federal Border Patrol surge ('Operation Charlotte's Web'): 'They say criminals, but it looks like they're just pulling people off the street' — public opposition to federal immigration enforcement, contrary to the federal-preemption position.

1.note: No deportation-policy statement found; the 287(g) termination was the sheriff's (2018), not hers.
